content.text: PRR Checklist for Claude Production Services

A Production Readiness Review (PRR) is a structured gate that a service must pass before it carries real user traffic. For Claude-based services the standard software PRR domains — reliability, observability, incident response — extend with Claude-specific concerns: prompt version control, output safety, token budget management, and model pin governance.
A PRR is not a one-time ceremony. Run it before each major launch and before any change that significantly alters traffic pattern, model version, or output schema.

This sub-subtopic provides a complete checklist across eight domains, with concrete criteria and the tests that verify each item. Every criterion maps to a specific observable or automated check — nothing is "judgment call only."

attrs.alt: PRR domain wheel showing eight review areas: reliability, safety, cost control, observability, security, incident response, capacity, and prompt versioning

content.text: Domain 1: Reliability
Reliability criteria establish what happens when the Claude API is slow, returns an error, or times out — and verify that the service degrades gracefully rather than cascading.
Criterion
How to verify
Retry logic with exponential backoff on 429/529
Inject 429 via a proxy mock; confirm backoff intervals double, max 3 attempts
Circuit breaker opens after N consecutive failures
Set a low threshold in staging and confirm the breaker opens and returns a local fallback
Timeout set on every Claude API call (not library default)
Read code; grep for timeout= in every client.messages.create call
Graceful fallback when Claude is unavailable
Kill the API in staging; confirm service returns a degraded (not broken) response
SLO defined: availability target and latency p99
SLO document exists and is linked from the service runbook
Error budget alert fires before SLO breach
Trigger synthetic errors; confirm PagerDuty alert fires before budget exhausted
Domain 2: Prompt Versioning
Claude-specific: every production service must use pinned, immutable prompt artifacts — not inline strings that change with each code deploy.
Criterion
How to verify
All prompts loaded from versioned artifacts (not hardcoded strings)
grep -r 'client.messages.create' in src/; confirm no inline system= string literals
Each prompt artifact has a SHA-256 pin in the deployment config
Read deploy/config/production.yaml; every prompt entry has sha256 field
Prompt artifact integrity verified at service startup
Read prompt_loader.py; confirm hash check runs before any request is served
Eval gate passed for current production version
CI history shows last publish tagged this version passing eval with score ≥ threshold
Rollback procedure documented and tested
Runbook exists; last rollback drill date < 90 days
Version reported in health endpoint
GET /internal/prompt-versions returns current name, version, sha256 for each prompt
Domain 3: Output Safety
For any service that returns Claude's output to end users, the PRR must verify that output filtering, content policy compliance, and adversarial robustness have been tested.
Criterion
How to verify
Output parsed and validated against a JSON schema (if structured output)
validate_output(response) called on every API response; schema file exists in artifact bundle
Jailbreak and prompt-injection test suite passed
Red-team results doc exists; last run date < 30 days; no P0/P1 unmitigated findings
User-supplied content never interpolated directly into system prompt
Audit code: all user input appears in user message only, never in system string
Output length bounded (max_tokens set, not library default)
grep for max_tokens in every messages.create call
Refusal rate monitored in production
Prometheus metric prompt_refusals_total exists; alert fires if rate exceeds 2× baseline
Sensitive PII not passed to the API unless required and documented
Data flow diagram exists; any PII fields are listed with justification
Domain 4: Cost Control
Unexpected token usage is the most common source of surprise bills for Claude-based services. The PRR must verify that budgets, per-request limits, and usage alerts are in place.
Criterion
How to verify
Token budget alert configured in Anthropic Console
Console shows workspace alert at 80% and 100% of monthly budget
max_tokens set explicitly on every API call
Code review; no call uses library default max_tokens
Input token count checked before large context calls
For calls with long context: token count logged; call aborted if over threshold
Per-user or per-tenant cost tracking implemented
Usage table exists in DB; anthropic_usage_tokens logged per request with user_id
Cost per request p95 within budget model
Load test results show p95 input+output tokens * price/token < budget
Cached prompt prefix used where calls share a long system prompt
Anthropic cache_control applied to static system prompt blocks

# Verify token budget before expensive context window calls
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
TOKEN_LIMIT = 50_000     # abort if context would exceed this

def count_tokens(system: str, messages: list[dict]) -> int:
    response = client.messages.count_tokens(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        system=system,
        messages=messages,
    )
    return response.input_tokens

def safe_call(system: str, messages: list[dict], max_tokens: int = 1024) -> str:
    n_tokens = count_tokens(system, messages)
    if n_tokens > TOKEN_LIMIT:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Context too large: {n_tokens} tokens (limit {TOKEN_LIMIT}). "
            "Summarise or truncate before calling."
        )
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=max_tokens,
        system=system,
        messages=messages,
    )
    return response.content[0].text
Domain 5: Observability
An observable Claude service emits structured traces for every API call, exposes dashboards for the key metrics, and has alerts that fire before users notice problems.
Criterion
How to verify
Structured log emitted for every Claude API call
Log includes: timestamp, prompt_name, version, input_tokens, output_tokens, latency_ms, error
Prometheus metrics exported: request count, error rate, latency, token usage
curl /metrics | grep prompt_ returns all four metric families
Dashboard exists for error rate, latency p50/p99, token usage/hr
Grafana dashboard URL in service README; last accessed within 7 days
Alert: error rate > 1% for 5 minutes
Alert rule exists in alertmanager config; tested with synthetic traffic
Alert: latency p99 > 5s for 5 minutes
Alert rule exists; silenced in non-production environments
Distributed trace propagates through Claude call
trace_id present in Claude call log; linked to upstream request trace

# Minimal observability wrapper — verify this pattern covers all call sites
import time, logging, json
from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
_requests = Counter("prompt_requests_total", "Claude API calls", ["prompt", "version"])
_errors   = Counter("prompt_errors_total",   "Claude API errors", ["prompt", "version", "type"])
_latency  = Histogram("prompt_latency_seconds", "Claude API latency", ["prompt", "version"])
_tokens   = Counter("prompt_tokens_total", "Tokens consumed", ["prompt", "version", "direction"])

def instrumented_call(client, prompt_name: str, version: str, **kwargs) -> object:
    _requests.labels(prompt=prompt_name, version=version).inc()
    t0 = time.monotonic()
    try:
        response = client.messages.create(**kwargs)
        elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
        _latency.labels(prompt=prompt_name, version=version).observe(elapsed)
        _tokens.labels(prompt=prompt_name, version=version, direction="input").inc(
            response.usage.input_tokens)
        _tokens.labels(prompt=prompt_name, version=version, direction="output").inc(
            response.usage.output_tokens)
        logger.info(json.dumps({
            "event": "claude_call",
            "prompt": prompt_name, "version": version,
            "latency_ms": round(elapsed * 1000, 1),
            "input_tokens": response.usage.input_tokens,
            "output_tokens": response.usage.output_tokens,
        }))
        return response
    except Exception as e:
        _errors.labels(prompt=prompt_name, version=version, type=type(e).__name__).inc()
        raise
Domain 6: Security
Criterion
How to verify
API key stored in secrets manager (not env var, not config file)
grep -r ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in src/; must not appear in source
API key rotated on schedule (≤ 90 days)
Rotation runbook exists; last rotation date in secrets manager metadata < 90 days
SBOM generated for every production build
CI pipeline produces CycloneDX SBOM; artifact stored in registry
Dependency vulnerability scan passes (no CRITICAL CVEs)
Grype/pip-audit report attached to last release; zero unmitigated CRITICAL findings
No user PII logged in Claude call logs
Log schema review: no email, IP, or full name fields in prompt call log entries
SLSA L2+ provenance for build artifacts
Build provenance file (attestation.json) in registry for last production build
Domain 7: Capacity and Quotas
Criterion
How to verify
Anthropic API rate limits documented for the workspace tier
Rate limit table in service runbook; matches current Console workspace plan
Load test run at 2× expected peak RPS
Load test report exists; no error rate spike, no circuit breaker trip at 2× load
Queue or throttling layer prevents burst traffic from exhausting rate limits
Architecture diagram shows rate-limiting component; config reviewed
Quota exhaustion behavior tested
Load test includes quota-exhausted scenario; service returns 429 with Retry-After, not 500
Auto-scaling policy set for upstream services that call Claude
HPA or similar config exists; scale-out tested in staging
Domain 8: Incident Response
Criterion
How to verify
On-call rotation covers all production hours
PagerDuty schedule shows no coverage gaps for the next 30 days
Runbook exists and is reachable from alert
Alert annotations include runbook_url; URL resolves
Rollback procedure tested within 90 days
Rollback drill log entry exists with date < 90 days
Mean time to rollback (MTTR-prompt) < 10 minutes
Last rollback log shows elapsed < 10 min from alert to confirmed version change
Incident communication template prepared
Status page template exists; last incident postmortem linked from runbook
Blast radius documented
Service card lists: max affected users, dependent services, data sensitivity class
PRR Scorecard and Sign-Off
The PRR produces a scored artifact that records the state of each domain at review time. A service is not approved for launch if any P0 criterion (marked below) is unmet:

# docs/prr/my-claude-service-2026-07-01.yaml
service: triage-api
review_date: "2026-07-01"
reviewers:
  - alice@example.com    # SRE
  - bob@example.com      # Security
  - carol@example.com    # Product

domains:
  reliability:
    score: 6/6
    p0_criteria_met: true
    notes: "Circuit breaker threshold set to 5 failures / 30s window"

  prompt_versioning:
    score: 6/6
    p0_criteria_met: true
    notes: "Last rollback drill: 2026-06-15 (7 min)"

  output_safety:
    score: 5/6
    p0_criteria_met: true
    open_items:
      - "PII data flow diagram: in progress (PR #451, due 2026-07-08)"

  cost_control:
    score: 6/6
    p0_criteria_met: true

  observability:
    score: 6/6
    p0_criteria_met: true

  security:
    score: 5/6
    p0_criteria_met: true
    open_items:
      - "SLSA L2 provenance: targeting next release cycle"

  capacity:
    score: 5/5
    p0_criteria_met: true

  incident_response:
    score: 6/6
    p0_criteria_met: true

decision: APPROVED
launch_date: "2026-07-08"
open_items_due: "2026-07-15"
All P0 criteria must be met before the launch date. Open items that are not P0 may be deferred to the post-launch window with a hard due date recorded in the scorecard.
Automating PRR Checks
Many PRR criteria are verifiable by script. Running automated checks before the review meeting saves time and eliminates human error on mechanical checks:

#!/bin/bash
# scripts/run_prr_checks.sh — run before PRR meeting
set -euo pipefail
FAILURES=0

check() {
  local name="$1"; shift
  if "$@" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "PASS: $name"
  else
    echo "FAIL: $name"
    FAILURES=$((FAILURES+1))
  fi
}

# Reliability
check "timeout set in all claude calls" \
  python3 scripts/check_claude_timeouts.py

# Prompt versioning
check "all prompts pinned in production config" \
  python3 scripts/check_prompt_pins.py

# Output safety
check "no system prompt f-strings" \
  python3 scripts/check_prompt_injection.py

# Cost
check "max_tokens set in all claude calls" \
  grep -rq "max_tokens=" src/

# Observability
check "prometheus metrics endpoint live" \
  curl -sf http://localhost:8000/metrics | grep -q "prompt_requests_total"

# Security
check "no api key in source" \
  ! grep -rq "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\s*=" src/

echo ""
if [ "$FAILURES" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "$FAILURES automated check(s) failed. Resolve before PRR."
  exit 1
fi
echo "All automated checks passed."
Summary
Eight domains:
reliability, prompt versioning, output safety, cost control, observability, security, capacity, incident response — all must be reviewed before launch.
P0 criteria:
retry logic, prompt pinning, SHA-256 integrity, output validation, secret management, and on-call coverage are non-negotiable.
Scorecard:
produce a YAML artifact for every review; record open items with owners and hard due dates.
Claude-specific additions:
standard software PRRs omit prompt versioning, output safety, token budget management, and model pin governance — add these explicitly.
Automated pre-checks:
run a script suite before the meeting to eliminate mechanical findings; spend review time on design decisions.
Recurring reviews:
re-run PRR before major model upgrades, traffic-pattern changes, and output schema changes — not just at first launch.
